zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Coney於1484年出生在英國林可爾史爾吉爾頓時，她的父親Sir William Kirton Lincolnshire Coney22歲，母親Alice Bell17歲。她於1512年與Sir John Lambert在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾吉爾頓結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1559年9月10日在英國英格蘭賀特福爾金也斯 瓦爾登享年75歲，埋葬在英國諾福爾可諾斯 艾爾哈姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Coney于1484年出生在英国林可爾史爾吉爾頓时，她的父亲Sir William Kirton Lincolnshire Coney22岁，母亲Alice Bell17岁。她于1512年与Sir John Lambert在英国英格兰林可爾史爾吉爾頓结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1559年9月10日在英国英格兰賀特福爾金也斯 瓦爾登享年75岁，埋葬在英国諾福爾可諾斯 艾爾哈姆。

nameMeanings.name: Coney
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English cony ‘rabbit’ (a back-formation from conies, from Old French conis, plural of conil), perhaps for someone thought to resemble a rabbit in some way or who wore rabbit skin or who sold rabbits or their skins. One York trader with this name in 1323 was a peleter or furrier.

variants.name: Cunning
meanings: Irish and Scottish (Fife): variant of Scottish or English Conning, from Middle English, Anglo-Norman French coning ‘rabbit’, used for someone thought to resemble a rabbit in some way; perhaps also an occupational name for a furrier. See also Coney .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Coney was born in 1484, in Kirton, Lincolnshire, England, her father, Sir William Kirton Lincolnshire Coney, was 22 and her mother, Alice Bell, was 17. She married Sir John Lambert in 1512, in Kirton, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 10 September 1559, in King's Walden, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 75, and was buried in North Elmham, Norfolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Coney est née en 1484, à Kirton, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir William Kirton Lincolnshire Coney, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Alice Bell, avait 17 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John Lambert en 1512, à Kirton, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 10 septembre 1559, à King's Walden, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 75 ans, et a été enterrée à North Elmham, Norfolk, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Coneyが1484年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアキルトンで生まれた時，父Sir William Kirton Lincolnshire Coneyは22歳，母Alice Bellは17歳であった。1512年にイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアキルトンでSir John Lambertと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1559年9月10日にイギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアキング'ス ワルデンで75歳で死去し，イギリスノーフォルクノース エルムハムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Coney(이)가 1484년 영국 링코실르 커턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William Kirton Lincolnshire Coney(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice Bell(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 1512년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 커턴에서 Sir John Lambert(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1559년 9월 10일 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 킨그'스 월든에서 75세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르폴크 노르스 엘함에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Coney родилась в 1484 г. в местности киртон, линколншир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir William Kirton Lincolnshire Coney было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Alice Bell, 17. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John Lambert в 1512 г. в местности киртон, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 10 сентября 1559 г. в местности кинг'с валден, хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 75 лет и была похоронена в местности норт элмхам, норфолк, Англия.
